---
title: "5a: Phylon Characterization"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Summary

Phylon characterization refines the NMF decomposition outputs from step 4a.
The L matrix (genes x phylons) is binarized via 3-means clustering, and
the A matrix (phylons x genomes) is binarized via a fixed threshold (>= 0.5).
Sorted visualizations reveal the phylon structure across genes and genomes.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
summary = pd.read_csv("../output/data/5a_phylon_summary.csv")
summary
```

## Gene Frequency vs Phylon Count

Linear regression showing the relationship between how frequently a gene
appears across genomes and the number of phylons it belongs to.

![](../output/figures/5a_regression.png){width=80%}

## Sorted L_binarized (Gene-Phylon Membership)

Genes sorted by: zero-phylon, single-phylon (per phylon), poly-phylon
(subclustered). Phylons ordered by dendrogram.

![](../output/figures/5a_L_binarized_sorted.png){width=80%}

## Sorted A_binarized (Phylon-Strain Affinity)

Strains sorted by: no-phylon, single-phylon, multi-phylon.
Phylons use the same dendrogram order as L.

![](../output/figures/5a_A_binarized_sorted.png){width=90%}
